chunk_summary: The PM POSHAN scheme in Indian government and aided schools provides daily nutrition through specified food group quantities, offering 450-700 kcal and 12-20 g protein per meal. It includes fortified inputs like rice, atta, iodized/double fortified salt, and fortified oil, enhancing micronutrient exposure but not guaranteeing adequacy.
key_facts: Evidence-based supportive care measures are essential
